cast.name: Rohit Kapoor
cast.notes: Wants honest status and can authorize descoping if engaged early.

private_drivers.coordination_implication: Bring Rohit the real blocker and descoped option early; do not ask for approval on vibes or fake green status.
cue_examples: He asks for the real plan, not a green tracker.
He responds well to early honest risk framing tied to a descoped pilot.
private_drivers.summary: Wants honest constraints early and will back a narrower credible pilot when the TPM brings the real tradeoff quickly.

cast.name: Maya Chen
cast.notes: Overloaded backend owner who resists fake commitments.

private_drivers.coordination_implication: Ask for honest feasibility and blockers first; avoid pressuring her for ETA theater while she is overloaded.
cue_examples: She explicitly says she will not give a fake ETA.
She responds better to honest feasibility asks than to date pressure.
private_drivers.summary: Low trust plus overload makes Maya push back hard on date pressure before the scope is real.

cast.name: Andrew Ortiz
cast.notes: Helpful frontend ally once scope is aligned.

cast.name: Rahul Iyer
cast.notes: Designer who needs support context before approving the descoped path.

cast.name: Nina Patel
cast.notes: Security reviewer with a useful-slot cutoff.

cast.name: Sara Feldman
cast.notes: Needs stable messaging for the customer-facing story.

private_drivers.coordination_implication: Give Sara a customer-safe plan before Wednesday lunch or the external story destabilizes.
cue_examples: The brief says the customer story has to settle early.
Sara asks for a stable story before lunch, not just more progress words.
private_drivers.summary: Needs a stable plan early so customer-facing messaging stays credible.

hidden_facts.summary: The current backend path is not credible for Friday.
The descoped pilot still needs security review.
Rohit wants honest constraints early and will back descoping when he sees a credible narrower path.
Maya reacts badly to fake date pressure while overloaded.
Design approval depends on support context.
Sara needs a stable customer story before Wednesday noon.

milestones.summary: A descoped pilot plan is aligned.
Security is engaged before the safe-slot cutoff.
Customer-facing plan is locked before the messaging window closes.

non_goals: Frontier-model calibration claims from one scenario
realism_notes: Pressure should come from timing, not melodrama.
The distraction path should be mildly useful but not gating.
Coworkers should have rational local incentives.
summary: A first-week TPM benchmark around rescuing a credible Friday customer pilot under hidden dependency and stakeholder pressure.
title: Northstar Launch Week
